tldr: Paul Magnier doubled up with his second Giro sprint win in three days, beating Jonathan Milan and Dylan Groenewegen in Sofia to close the Bulgarian Grande Partenza. The lone cat-2 climb (Borovets Pass) was never going to disturb the sprinter scenario; Magnier kept the pink jersey for the rest day transfer to Italy.

narrative.headline: Magnier's second win, second pink
narrative.body: 175 km from Plovdiv to Sofia. Flat opening, one cat-2 climb (Borovets Pass, 9.2 km at 5.3%), descent and run-in to Sofia. The break formed early and was caught with kilometres to spare; the GC contenders stayed safe in the front of the bunch through to the sprint.

gcImpact.headline: Magnier keeps pink
gcImpact.body: Bonus seconds on the stage win and intermediate sprint keep Soudal's young sprinter in pink across the rest day transfer to Italy.

riderPerformances.name: Paul Magnier
riderPerformances.narrative: Doubled up in Sofia, taking his second bunch sprint in three days to close out the Bulgarian opening before the transfer to Italy. Back-to-back wins established Magnier and Soudal Quick-Step as the dominant sprint force of week one and put him firmly into the maglia ciclamino fight.

keyMoments.what: Second sprint win of the race, in Sofia

riderPerformances.name: Jonathan Milan
riderPerformances.narrative: Second on the Sofia sprint. Magnier's lead-out was cleaner; Milan's Lidl-Trek train still working through early-Giro chemistry.
